name: PetVivo Holdings, Inc.
business: PetVivo Holdings, Inc. is an emerging biomedical device company focused on the manufacturing, commercialization, and licensing of innovative medical devices and therapeutics for animals. Its lead product, Spryng with OsteoCushion Technology, is a veterinarian-administered intraarticular injection for the management of lameness and osteoarthritis in dogs and horses. The company also markets and sells PrecisePRP (Platelet-Rich Plasma) product for both canine and equine under an exclusive licensing agreement with VetStem, Inc.

exposures.sentence: PetVivo Holdings, Inc. (the Company, PetVivo, we or us) is an emerging biomedical device company focused on the manufacturing, commercialization, and licensing of innovative medical devices and therapeutics for animals.

The Company began commercialization of its lead product Spryng with OsteoCushion Technology, a veterinarian-administered, intraarticular injection for the management of lameness and other joint afflictions such as osteoarthritis in dogs and horses, in the second quarter of its fiscal year ended March 31, 2022.

catalysts.event: Second ISO cleanroom facility expected operational
catalysts.sentence: A second ISO cleanroom facility is expected to be operational later this year.

catalysts.event: Second canine clinical study expected completion
catalysts.sentence: the second began in June of 2023 with an expected completion in October 2024.

catalysts.event: Purchase Option for up to $1.5 million investment by July 15, 2026
catalysts.sentence: an investor shareholder has a Purchase Option to invest up to $1.5 million into the company by July 15, 2026.

tamClaims.claim: Osteoarthritis affects approximately 14 million dogs and 1 million horses in the $11 billion companion animal veterinary care and product sales market.
tamClaims.sentence: Osteoarthritis affects approximately 14 million dogs and 1 million horses in the $11 billion companion animal veterinary care and product sales market.

tamClaims.claim: The American Pet Products Association (APPA) 2021-2022 National Pet Owners Survey indicates that $123.6 billion was spent on pets in the U. S. in 2021. Vet Care and product sales constitute about $34.3 billion of the market.
tamClaims.sentence: The American Pet Products Association (APPA) 2021-2022 National Pet Owners Survey indicates that $123.6 billion was spent on pets in the U. S. in 2021. Vet Care and product sales constitute about $34.3 billion of the market.

tamClaims.claim: The Morris Animal Foundation estimates that OA affects approximately 14 million adult dogs in the U.S.
tamClaims.sentence: The Morris Animal Foundation estimates that OA affects approximately 14 million adult dogs in the U.S. and owners consistently report it as a top concern.
